everyone.name: Dolis Sharma
everyone.bio: Dolis Sharma is a seasoned technologist with extensive experience guiding enterprises through their cloud-native journeys. She has deep expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, DevOps, and DevSecOps, helping organizations accelerate and scale cloud-native adoption. Dolis has built and managed mission-critical systems and successfully led multiple environments through rigorous security certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Known for translating complex technical challenges into tangible business outcomes, she partners closely with enterprise teams, offering architecture guidance, automation strategies, and operational best practices. When she’s not exploring the world of technology, she enjoys hiking, chasing waterfalls, relaxing on the beach, or dancing. She holds a diploma in Kathak and loves moving to the rhythm of the music

everyone.name: Bmk Lakshminarayanan
everyone.bio: Transformation Architect, SECTION6 BMK is an inspiring and passionate DevOps Advocate and Value Stream Architect promoting DevOps, Lean and Value Stream Management principles & practices. He is also a New Zealand ambassador for the Cloud-Native Computing Foundation(CNCF), DevOps Institute and a Board Advisor for the Value Stream Management consortium(VSMC). He hosts the CNCF meetup in Wellington, New Zealand and is co-chair of Cloud-Native Summit A/NZ. He blogs about his experiences and learnings at https://bmk.nz/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: 1. Promoting Open Source and Cloud-Native projects and ecosystem tools in our community. 2. Connecting with experts, maintainers, contributors and other Opensource and cloud-native thought leaders to amplify my learning 3. Use the ambassador program and help in bringing in the best to my community in terms of cloud-native learnings, education and training.

everyone.name: Matthias Loibl
everyone.bio: Matthias is the Director of PolarSignals Cloud. He maintains Prometheus, the Prometheus Operator, Parca, Pyrra, and more. He is an emeritus Thanos maintainer. He organized PromCon (the Prometheus conference) in 2023 and 2024. He runs the Berlin Prometheus MeetUp.

everyone.name: Lustenberg
everyone.bio: Avid Linux on Desktop user, admin, and developer with over 25 years experience across physical on prem, cloud, and container / serverless technologies.  When not wrangling servers, Alex spends his time SCUBA cave diving, and fishing for electrons as an Amateur Radio operator.

everyone.name: Haruki Nogi
everyone.bio: I am a system architect and work with clients in the financial industry. I have a passion in cloud-native technologies centered around Kubernetes (DevOps, observability, chaos engineering, container security, etc.).

everyone.name: Justin Watulingas
everyone.bio: I'm a Platform Engineer and Kubernetes enjoyer focused on building robust Cloud Native platforms that balance performance, automation, and security. I enjoy simplifying complex systems and creating reliable foundations that help engineering teams deliver with confidence. Staying current with emerging tools, security practices, and platform patterns is a key part of my approach. I actively pursue opportunities to deepen my expertise through continuous learning and aim to create solutions that are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with long-term organizational needs.

ambassadors.name: Dolis Sharma
ambassadors.bio: Dolis Sharma is a seasoned technologist with extensive experience guiding enterprises through their cloud-native journeys. She has deep expertise in Kubernetes, GitOps, DevOps, and DevSecOps, helping organizations accelerate and scale cloud-native adoption. Dolis has built and managed mission-critical systems and successfully led multiple environments through rigorous security certifications, including SOC 2 and ISO 27001. Known for translating complex technical challenges into tangible business outcomes, she partners closely with enterprise teams, offering architecture guidance, automation strategies, and operational best practices. When she’s not exploring the world of technology, she enjoys hiking, chasing waterfalls, relaxing on the beach, or dancing. She holds a diploma in Kathak and loves moving to the rhythm of the music

ambassadors.name: Rohit Ghumare
ambassadors.bio: Rohit Ghumare is a recognized Google Developer Expert in AI and Cloud, Docker Captain, Platform Ambassador, CNCF Marketing Chair 2025 a testament to his adeptness in contemporary cloud solutions. Contributed to Platform Engineer Associate exam and Certified Backstage Exam. He is the driving force behind several community-centric initiatives, orchestrating events as the organizer of KCD UK, Cloud Native Westminster London, DevOpsDays Bengaluru, KCD Mumbai, Google Cloud Mumbai, and the Cloud Native Thane community group. Through these platforms, he not only showcases his profound expertise but also fosters a collaborative ecosystem for knowledge exchange. A prolific writer, Rohit has authored a plethora of blogs and insightful pieces on CNCF projects, including, but not limited to, Backstage, Istio, Kubernetes, Cilium, Envoy, Prometheus, and Kiali. This extensive coverage underscores his profound understanding and engagement with cutting-edge cloud-native technologies. Besides penning down his knowledge, Rohit is dedicated to sharing his insights through the "Cloud Native Talks" podcast, which he hosts, and through global conferences and local meetups where he's presented multiple talks. He has also been a program committee member for Kubecon co-located events. Links: devopsguys.inWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Being appointed as an ambassador presents a remarkable opportunity to further extend the outreach of my engagement within the tech community, particularly in the realms of DevOps and Cloud Native technologies. It will help me to give back to the community by sharing insights from experts located all over the world.

ambassadors.name: Michael O'Leary
ambassadors.bio: I'm a Solution Architect at F5 by day, and I organize the Boston Kubernetes Meetup. I enjoy helping customers solve K8s challenges, usually around networking and security. I'm a dad of 2 fantastic boys and I blog at https://michaeloleary.net

ambassadors.name: Faisal Afzal
ambassadors.bio: As a Solutions Architect specializing in cloud-native technologies, I’m committed to helping organizations leverage the power of Kubernetes, DevOps, and containerization to drive innovation and efficiency. I lead the CNCF Los Angeles chapter, where we host meetups, webinars, and workshops to engage the community in learning and collaboration around cloud-native best practices. With a strong background in cloud migration, automation, and modernization, I strive to connect people with the latest insights in the cloud-native space, encouraging skill growth and knowledge-sharing within an inclusive community. Visit and become a member at https://community.cncf.io/los-angeles/

ambassadors.name: Niki Manoledaki
ambassadors.bio: Niki Manoledaki is a senior software engineer, environmental sustainability advocate, keynote speaker, and community facilitator. Her work focuses on managing cloud resources efficiently as part of the platform engineering team at Grafana Labs and as a co-chair of the CNCF Environmental Sustainability TAG's Green Reviews Working Group.

ambassadors.name: Fabrizio Sgura
ambassadors.bio: Committed to innovation and new technologies, strong believer in cloud native and container orchestration. Participating in main events, both as speaker and attendant, actively spreading the CNCF principles and project. My job is my favorite game.

ambassadors.name: Marcus Noble
ambassadors.bio: Marcus is a platform engineer and his main area of focus in recent years has been around CI/CD, Go, Kubernetes, containers and DevOps but originally started out as a web developer and JavaScript enthusiast. A self-described “tinkerer”, when not building Kubernetes solutions, Marcus likes to dabble with 3D printing and experimenting with smart home tech. He can be found blogging online at www.MarcusNoble.co.uk and giving talks at meetups & conferences.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I am excited to leverage the support of the CNCF to better help others within out community through shared knowledge and experience. I'm excited for the opportunity to meet more amazing people in the cloud native space and learn from their experiences and expertise.

ambassadors.name: Hung-Ying Tai
ambassadors.bio: I am the maintainer of WasmEdge Runtime and an active mentor in LFX mentorship. I am enthusiastic about sharing how to leverage the WebAssembly technology with the Cloud Native ecosystem, including giving talks at the conference[1] and hosting online tutorials [2]. [1]: https://kccnceu2023.sched.com/event/6c0f559281122a74f5292f3d05b5936e?iframe=no#video_stream [2]: https://coscup.org/2023/zh-TW/session/EFYC8R I am going to have more tutorials and information related to Cloud Native+Wasm on my personal blog (https://hyd.ai). Stay tuned!Why I am excited to be an ambassador: WebAssembly is new for the Cloud Native ecosystem. I’m excited to join the CNCF ambassador community this year to promote the connection between Could Native software and Wasm and bring this knowledge to local communities and university clubs/courses in Taiwan.

kubestronauts.name: Lustenberg
kubestronauts.bio: Avid Linux on Desktop user, admin, and developer with over 25 years experience across physical on prem, cloud, and container / serverless technologies.  When not wrangling servers, Alex spends his time SCUBA cave diving, and fishing for electrons as an Amateur Radio operator.

kubestronauts.name: Diego Arce
kubestronauts.bio: I'm a Cloud Solutions Architect with over 11 years in software engineering and cloud infrastructure. Alongside my role as a Staff Software Engineer at Zuora Inc., I'm an enthusiastic advocate of cloud technologies, Kubernetes, containers, and serverless computing—I can't imagine life without coding! In my free time, I love experimenting with IoT, Arduinos, and Raspberry Pis, and I'm always up for a tech conference.

kubestronauts.name: Kelvin Li
kubestronauts.bio: A Tech lover. Keep learning CNCF technologies.

kubestronauts.name: Md. Rakibul Hassan
kubestronauts.bio: Working as a Head of Platform Architecture and DevOps Engineering. I lead the strategic design and implementation of scalable, secure, and high-performing platforms. My role bridges technical architecture and operational excellence, enabling seamless software delivery and robust system reliability. I specialize in cloud-native solutions, CI/CD automation, and incident management while fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement across teams.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Lavanya Anbalagan
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am an enthusiastic learner with over five years of experience in enterprise container platforms and holds a Kubestronaut title. In addition to my professional achievements, I have a personal interest in exploring new places and maintaining physical fitness.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Gabriel Machado
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm a software engineer with over a decade of professional experience, specialized in cloud native solutions and public cloud providers. Over my career, I've designed and maintained scalable applications across multiple industries.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Sergei Kubin
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Sergei is an ICT Engineer with over 20 years of experience in enterprise hardware and software support. His primary focus area is data storage systems. He is an IBM RedBooks author with more than 10 published books.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Piotr Korlaga
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm a Software Engineer Consultant with a ten-year track record of building IT solutions for various sectors e.g.: public, energy, telecom, finance and retail & petrol. Well-versed in all phases of the software development lifecycle. I strongly believe in Agile, engineering culture and automation what is proven way to bring business value to customers.
